message.content:
You are the ADVERSARIAL GATE for an autonomous research-agent flywheel. Your job is to REFUTE, not to agree. Prior gates of yours have twice caught the driver reporting a measurement artifact as a finding — do it again if it is there. Be concrete and cite file:line. Do not be agreeable.

Repo: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel (branch flywheel-v1, HEAD 6a5112e). Read source before accepting any claim.

CONTEXT. The system composes a long-form research report from an evidence corpus (997 rows). A two-pass semantic "topic judge" was built (Rank4/Rank5) that deletes rows it affirmatively verdicts OFF_SUBJECT in BOTH independently shuffled passes. It deletes 507/997 (~51%) and reproduces that number across 4 independent invocations. The mission: beat frontier deep-research systems (10-22k word reports); we are at ~4k.

THE EXPERIMENT JUST RUN (A/B, judge ON vs OFF, same corpus cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json, same DRB task 72):
- CTRL (dirty pool): 10 sections, 3408 words, 81 verified / 75 dropped sentences, 63 bibliography entries.
  outputs at /home/polaris/wt/fw_ctrl/outputs/rank6_ctrl_compose/ (log: /home/polaris/wt/fw_ctrl/rank6_ctrl_compose.log)
- ARMED (clean pool, 559 rows deleted): 10 sections, 4489 words, 104 verified / 68 dropped, 72 bibliography entries.
  outputs at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank6_armed_compose/ (log: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/logs/rank6_armed_compose.log)

MY FOUR CLAIMS — attack each. Tell me which are artifacts.

CLAIM 1 (I killed my own prior thesis): "The relevance payoff of cleaning the pool is ~ZERO." I had argued a ~51%-alien evidence pool poisons the shipped prose. I measured the shipped BIBLIOGRAPHY instead: the DIRTY report cites 1 off-subject row out of 63 (1.6%); the clean one cites 0 of 72. I concluded the writer was already discarding alien rows at citation time, so cleaning buys ~one citation of relevance. METHOD: I took the 507 off_subject_ev_ids from outputs/rank6_armed_compose/topic_judge_dispositions.json, walked bibliography.json for ev_id/evidence_id fields, plus regex ev_\d+ over report.md, and intersected. IS THIS MEASUREMENT SOUND? Specifically: (a) does bibliography.json actually key rows by the same ev_id space as the judge's corpus ids, or could the bibliography be keyed by a re-indexed/renumbered id so my intersection is vacuous and would report ~0 overlap NO MATTER WHAT? (b) Both arms live-retrieve extra rows at gap-fill (CTRL evidence_rows=1002, ARMED=1021) which get FRESH ids outside the judge's 997-row space — could alien rows be entering the bibliography with ids the judge never verdicted, so my "1.6% alien" is an undercount and the real alien share of shipped citations is much higher? Verify by actually reading titles of the cited rows, not by trusting my number. If my relevance conclusion is wrong the whole prioritisation flips, so this is the most important thing you check.

CLAIM 2 (I refused to claim a win): Fable (you, earlier) predicted |delta kept_fraction| <= 0.05 because strict_verify is topicality-blind (provenance_generator.py:3598 never takes the research question; entailment_judge.py:740 judge(sentence, span) is prompted only "Decide whether the SPAN entails the SENTENCE"). Observed 0.519 -> 0.605 = +0.085. I refused to call this a refutation: kept_fraction is a proportion over ~160 sentence trials, SE(diff) ~ sqrt(2*0.55*0.45/160) ~ 0.055, so +0.085 is ~1.5 sigma = noise at n=1 per arm. IS MY STATISTICS RIGHT? Is the binomial model even appropriate (sentences within a section are NOT independent draws — they share a menu, a prompt, and a repair loop; does clustering make my SE too SMALL, i.e. is the true uncertainty even bigger)? Am I right to log this UNRESOLVED rather than as a win?

CLAIM 3 (my reconciliation): "The clean pool helps the WRITER, not the VERIFIER." Evidence: repair-loop success 0.71 -> 0.87, verified sentences 81 -> 104. Mechanism I propose: strict_verify really is topicality-blind, but when the writer is forced to build an ON-TOPIC section from an ALIEN row, it writes a sentence that OVERREACHES its span (an unsupported bridge), and entailment then fails on faithfulness grounds — no topic-awareness required. So your earlier inference ("topicality cannot affect kept_fraction") carried a hidden assumption: that the sentences WRITTEN are the same regardless of pool. IS THIS MECHANISM REAL OR A JUST-SO STORY? Can you find direct evidence in the logs (dropped sentence texts, repair_loop lines, per-section kept_fraction) that discriminates it from the alternative explanation: that the +32% word gain is simply MENU SIZE — multi_section_generator.py:513-541 records that oversized writer menus (52-103 rows) caused a kept_fraction collapse to 0.35, and PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION (:541, the purpose-built menu cap) has NEVER been switched on. If it is menu size, then the cheap lever is the cap (free) and NOT the 42-minute judge, and my whole ranking is wrong.

CLAIM 4 (the ceiling lever, now running): Rank7 (commit 33108d8) made the section sentence target tunable: PG_SECTION_SENTENCE_TARGET / PG_SECTION_DISTINCT_SOURCES applied at the single _select_section_system_prompt seam (multi_section_generator.py:3903). I verified both templates emit "28-36 sentences", both copies of rule #8 moved, no stale "10-18" survives, and the other bands (20-35/15-20/10-15) are inert because they are inside the Mechanism-section rule gated on section title == "Mechanism" (no such section here). A run is live now with 28-36 / 15 distinct sources on the clean pool. WHAT WILL BREAK? Consider specifically: B11 same-span dedup (verified_compose.dedup_same_span_sentences) keeps only ONE sentence per distinct (ev_id,start,end) span — so if sections do not HAVE 15+ distinct usable sources, does raising the sentence target just produce sentences that get dedup'd or dropped, making the report SHORTER (the project already measured "more rows => thinner prose")? Is there a per-section kept_fraction FLOOR with retry (I saw "post-M-41c kept_fraction=0.31 below min 0.40 — retrying") that will now thrash or burn budget? Predict the failure mode CONCRETELY and give me the number to look at first when it lands.

Return: a verdict per claim (CONFIRMED / REFUTED / ARTIFACT / UNPROVEN), the file:line evidence, and — most important — the ONE measurement I should make next that would most change the ranking of what to do. Be brutal.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

        from src.utils.shared_ssl_context import get_shared_ssl_context
        return httpx.Client(
            verify=get_shared_ssl_context(),
            timeout=httpx.Timeout(
                connect=_ENTAILMENT_CONNECT_S,
                read=_ENTAILMENT_READ_STALL_S,
                write=_ENTAILMENT_WRITE_S,
                pool=_ENTAILMENT_POOL_S,
            ),
            limits=httpx.Limits(
                max_keepalive_connections=_ENTAILMENT_MAX_KEEPALIVE,
                keepalive_expiry=_ENTAILMENT_KEEPALIVE_EXPIRY_S,
            ),
        )

    def judge(self, sentence: str, span: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
        """Return (verdict, reason) — the public entry, with the I3 verdict idempotency cache.

        I-deepfix-001 fix I3: a process-local cache keyed on (model, normalized sentence, normalized
        span). On a hit the cached (verdict, reason) is returned WITHOUT a network call — the SAME tuple
        the judge already produced for this exact input (temperature=0.0 => idempotent), so every
        downstream strict_verify / provenance decision + telemetry is byte-identical. On a miss the real
        judge (`_judge_uncached`) runs and its verdict is cached — EXCEPT the fail-CLOSED
        ('ENTAILED','judge_error:…') sentinel, which is never cached so a transient fault stays retryable.
        Default-ON; ``PG_JUDGE_VERDICT_CACHE=0`` makes this wrapper a pass-through (byte-identical pre-I3).
        Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: the cache can only echo a verdict the judge itself returned; it can never
        manufacture, relax, or flip one. The two-family/model lock + fail-closed contract are untouched."""
        # I3 P1 (Fable gate iter1): the verdict is a function of the RESOLVED entailment prompt, which is a
        # call-time variable (PG_ENTAILMENT_PROMPT_VARIANT). Include the active variant in the cache key so
        # a same-process bakeoff can never be served one variant's verdict for another variant's prompt.
        variant = _active_prompt_variant()
        cached = _verdict_cache.get(self._model, sentence, span, variant)
        if cached is not None:
            return cached
        verdict, reason = self._judge_uncached(sentence, span)
        # `put` refuses the fail-closed sentinel itself (defensive), so a transient judge_error is never
        # memoized — a later identical call re-issues a fresh attempt at a real verdict.
        _verdict_cache.put(self._model, sentence, span, verdict, reason, variant)
        return verdict, reason

    def _judge_uncached(self, sentence: str, span: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
        """Return (verdict, reason) — the UNCACHED judge call (the I3 cache wraps this in `judge`).

        verdict is one of "ENTAILED", "NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED".

        I-transport-001 (#1191) Site 4: a transient transport/parse/empty-choices/bad-verdict

# much of a facet's matched evidence can render REGARDLESS of how much verified evidence exists — a
# facet with 40 matched rows keeps only 30. That is a FILTER-AND-CAP the §-1.3 DNA bans. When this
# flag is ON, the per-section cap TRACKS the section's full matched payload (the ``min(.., 30)``
# ceiling is dropped); the SACRED per-facet reserved set is still never truncated, and the default
# char-budget path (I-arch-005) is unchanged (it already dissolves the row cap into a 120K-char
# compute bound). Read at CALL time (monkeypatch-testable). Default-OFF => the exact legacy row-cap
# ceiling => byte-identical.
def _ev_budget_tracks_payload() -> bool:
    """True iff PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD removes the PG_MAX_EV_PER_SECTION row-cap CEILING so a
    section's evidence budget tracks its full matched payload (F2 cap-removal). Default-OFF."""
    return os.getenv("PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD", "0").strip().lower() not in (
        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
    )


# ── RACE-FLOOR lever 2 (#1344 R2): WRITER-MENU top-N ev-density cap ───────────────────────────────
# THE DRAG (drb_72 R2 render, MEASURED): with PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS + PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD ON,
# route_all/facet-route APPEND ~orphan tails so each body section's ``ev_ids`` balloons to 52-103 rows
# (vs the deep step3 run's ~15/section). The abstractive writer is then prompted over that crammed
# menu and strict_verify DROPS ~65% of what it writes (post-M-41c kept_fraction=0.35) -> THIN prose
# (2141 body words vs step3's 3230) and 102 dropped sentences. This cap FOCUSES the WRITER's PROMPT
# menu to the top-N highest-ranked rows (``section.ev_ids`` is already ranked: reserved-facet +
# authority-ordered head first, route_all/facet-route orphans appended to the TAIL), so the writer
# composes deep, well-grounded prose over the primaries exactly like the step3 draw.
#
# §-1.3 / FAITHFULNESS (why this is provably SAFE — not a source drop, not a verify weakening):
#   * It caps ONLY ``ev_subset`` — the rows the WRITER is PROMPTED with. It does NOT touch
#     ``section.ev_ids`` (bibliography + credibility disclosure are built from that, so every routed
#     row STAYS cited-eligible + disclosed) and does NOT touch ``evidence_pool``.
#   * strict_verify / ``_rewrite_draft_with_spans`` gate every emitted sentence against the FULL
#     ``evidence_pool`` (NOT ``ev_subset``) — so a capped-out row can still GROUND a sentence the
#     writer happened to write, and no sentence is ever admitted that the full pool cannot verify.
#     The frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / [#calc] / fold-in) is UNTOUCHED.
#   * The selection is DETERMINISTIC (head-N of the already-ranked list) and DISCLOSED (LOUD
#     activation log naming the withheld count per section).
# Default-OFF (``PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION`` unset / <=0) => ``ev_subset`` is the full assigned
# set => byte-identical legacy render.
def _writer_topn_ev_per_section() -> int:
    """Top-N cap on the per-section WRITER evidence menu (``ev_subset``). ``PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION``
    read at call time (monkeypatch-testable). <=0 / non-integer => 0 => the cap is OFF (byte-identical:
    the writer sees every assigned row)."""
    try:
        v = int(os.getenv("PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION", "0").strip())
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return 0
    return v if v > 0 else 0


def _apply_writer_menu_cap(
    ev_subset: list, *, section_title: str = "", total_assigned: int = 0
) -> list:
    """Return the top-N head of ``ev_subset`` (the WRITER prompt menu) per
    ``_writer_topn_ev_per_section()``. Returns a NEW list (never mutates the input, so the caller's
    ``section.ev_ids`` / ``evidence_pool`` are never touched). Cap<=0 or a shorter menu => the input
    list unchanged (byte-identical). Discloses the withheld tail LOUD when it bites."""
    cap = _writer_topn_ev_per_section()

    only by ``agents/synthesizer.py`` / ``synthesis/section_writer.py``, which this module does not
    import. Report length is emergent: n_sections x rule-#8's sentence target x ~30 words/sentence.

    as the binding CEILING: evidence-rich sections already render 16-18 sentences, i.e. the top of the
    band. This makes that band the single tunable it always was.

    Rule #9 moves WITH rule #8 on purpose. The B11 same-span dedup pass
    (``verified_compose.dedup_same_span_sentences``, default ON) keeps only one sentence per distinct
    ``(ev_id, start, end)`` footprint, so extra sentences that re-word an ALREADY-CITED span are
    deleted. Raising the sentence target without raising the distinct-source floor would invite the
    model to pad from the same spans and B11 would silently eat the gain — the exact no-op shape that
    killed Rank6. The lever is "cite more DISTINCT sources", not "write more sentences".

    Unset => the ORIGINAL template object is returned unchanged (same identity), so the locked
    benchmark is byte-identical. Set => FAILS LOUD if an anchor drifted, so a future template edit
    cannot silently turn this into a no-op."""

    sources = os.getenv(_DISTINCT_SOURCES_ENV, "").strip()
    if not target and not sources:
        return template

    out = template
    if target:

        if n != 1:

                f"{_SENTENCE_TARGET_ENV} set but the rule-#8 anchor {_RULE8_ANCHOR!r} fired {n} "

        if n != 1:

All evidence gathered. Here is the gate verdict.

---

# ADVERSARIAL GATE VERDICT — Rank6 A/B + Rank7

## CLAIM 1 — "Relevance payoff of cleaning is ~zero": **CONFIRMED** (independently recomputed; your method had two latent holes that happened not to bite)

I did not trust your intersection — I rebuilt it padding-safe from raw files.

**(a) Id-space vacuity — NO.** The corpus (`cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json`, 997 rows) uses ids `ev_000`–`ev_1270` (992 ev_N + 5 slugs like `acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks`). All 507 `off_subject_ev_ids` in `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank6_armed_compose/topic_judge_dispositions.json` resolve into that corpus (507/507 after normalization). Bibliography top-level `evidence_id` is in the SAME space (59/63 CTRL, 67/72 ARMED are ev_N in corpus). Not renumbered. My independent, zero-pad-normalized intersection reproduces your numbers exactly: **CTRL cites 1 off-subject row (entry [46], ev_852, "How Interest Groups Influence Public Policy — UMSV Online", cited once in prose), ARMED cites 0.** Two method holes you got away with: (i) your `ev_\d+` regex over report.md was structurally vacuous — prose uses `[n]` markers, and both summaries record `leaked_cite_ev_tokens: 0`, so that leg of your method could never find anything; (ii) the corpus zero-pads ids under 1000 (`ev_050`) — a raw string intersection would silently miss any unpadded variant. Neither changed the answer this time. Say so in the log.

**(b) Fresh-row undercount — REAL but SMALL, and it cuts AGAINST the clean arm.** Corpus max is ev_1270. CTRL cites 1 unjudged fresh row (ev_1271, on-topic title). ARMED cites **3** (ev_1283, ev_1290, ev_1291). So the "much higher hidden alien share" fear is refuted — ceiling is 3/72 = 4%. BUT: read ARMED bibliography entries #1 and #2. ev_1290's verified "claim_text" is a chopped reference-list scrap (`"on and sustainable growth. _Technol. Forecast. Soc. Change_ 162, 120398..."`) marked ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED/SUPPORTS; ev_1291 is `basket_verdict: "unverified"` (weight 0.45) and is still bibliography entry #2, cited in prose. **The clean report's two lead citations are garbage-quality gap-fill rows the judge never saw.** Your relevance metric is blind to this; it is a quality problem, not a topicality problem, and it shipped in the ARMED arm.

Title-level audit of all 63 CTRL entries: only ev_852 is alien. Your 1.6% stands under direct inspection. The prioritisation flip you feared is not there — the payoff conclusion holds.

## CLAIM 2 — "+0.085 is unresolved noise": **CONFIRMED, and your SE is too SMALL — plus a bias you missed**

Premise verified in source: `strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, ...)` takes no research question (`src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:3598`); `judge(self, sentence: str, span: str)` at `src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:741` sees only sentence+span. Counts verified: CTRL 81/156 = 0.519, ARMED 104/172 = 0.605.

Your binomial SE (~0.055) is a **lower bound**. Sentences cluster within 10 sections sharing a menu, a draft, and a repair loop — with any positive ICC the design effect inflates the true SE, so +0.085 is even less than your 1.5σ.

The bias you missed: the retry floor **selects the better of two drafts** (`multi_section_generator.py:6593`: `if len(report2_kept_after_m41c) > post_filter_kept`). Per the logs, ARMED fired this best-of-two retry in **3 sections** (first-pass kept_fractions 0.38, 0.31, 0.29 — `logs/rank6_armed_compose.log:2076,2087,2121`); CTRL fired it **zero** times. Part of the +0.085 is mechanical retry selection applied asymmetrically. UNRESOLVED is the right log entry; if anything the delta is inflated.

Note what those 3 ARMED retries also tell you: **the clean pool still collapsed below the 0.40 floor in 3 of 10 sections.** Cleanliness did not fix the kept_fraction failure mode.

## CLAIM 3 — "Clean pool helps the WRITER": **ARTIFACT-RISK / UNPROVEN — the menu-size confound is live and unmeasured, and it is large**

The decisive numbers, from the two `multi_section_outline.json` files — per-section writer menus:

| | CTRL | ARMED |
|---|---|---|
| menus | 99, 195, 82, 45, 45, 34, 33, 12, 52, **358** | 45, 61, 39, 70, 37, 24, 8, 11, 14, **156** |

The judge halved the menus. The project's own prior measurement (`multi_section_generator.py:521-525`) says menus of 52-103 rows alone — no alien pool involved — collapse kept_fraction to 0.35 and thin prose. CTRL ran 4 sections above that band, one at 358 rows. And the 120K char-budget did NOT save it: zero `char_budget_exceeded` / "char-budget bound" lines in either log (the budget is only applied `if _redesign`, line 2276/2653 — it was 0 here), so the writer genuinely saw 195- and 358-row menus. **Your A/B changed pool cleanliness and menu size simultaneously. It cannot discriminate your overreach mechanism from menu overload.** I found no sentence-level evidence for the overreach story either — drop-reason lines in the logs are too sparse (8 verify_fail + 4 no_provenance_token visible in CTRL) to support it. It is currently a just-so story sitting on top of a confound, and the confounded variable has the stronger prior — measured, in this codebase, at this effect size. `PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION` (`multi_section_generator.py:541`) remains OFF and free.

## CLAIM 4 — Rank7 prediction: **code CONFIRMED, outcome will be dedup/floor-limited in tail sections — here is the concrete failure**

Code verified: `_apply_depth_targets` (`multi_section_generator.py:~3905`) rewrites both anchors with `re.subn` count-enforced fail-loud, including the Mechanism back-reference. The seam is sound.

The failure mode: ARMED-shaped tail sections have menus of **8, 11, 14 rows** (Policy, Cross-Study, Conclusions). Rule #9 will demand 15 distinct sources from an 8-row menu — unsatisfiable by construction. The writer will pad 28-36 sentences by re-citing the same spans; B11 (`verified_compose.py:155`, keep-one-per-(ev_id,start,end)-footprint, number carve-out only) eats the rewords. At the 10-18 target B11 collapsed only 1 sentence per run (logs, both arms); at 28-36 it becomes the binding constraint in small-menu sections. Then post-M-41c kept_fraction lands below 0.40 and the floor fires — but it is a **single** retry with best-of-two, not a thrash loop (6538-6593; `regen_attempted` once), so budget burn is bounded at ~2x generation per section, not runaway.

**Concrete prediction:** rich sections (37-70 rows) gain real length; the 3 tail sections plateau at roughly menu-size-to-2x-menu-size kept sentences regardless of target. Expect total verified ~140-170, dropped sentences roughly doubling, and 4-6 "below min 0.40 — retrying" lines. **The number to read first when it lands:** the count of `B11 same-span dedup: collapsed N` per section, next to that section's menu size — if collapsed-N scales with (sentence_target − menu_size), the ceiling is source diversity per section and the next lever is section-level retrieval/routing, not the prompt band. (As of the log tail, the Rank7 run is still in the judge phase — 0 retry lines yet.)

## THE ONE MEASUREMENT

**Run a third arm: DIRTY pool + `PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION=15`. No judge.** ~40 min, $0 new machinery. It splits Claim 3 cleanly: if kept_fraction and verified-sentence count match ARMED (~0.60 / ~104), the 42-minute judge is off the critical path entirely — the whole cleanliness program reduces to a free env var, your ranking inverts, and Rank7's depth lever should be stacked on the cap, not the judge. If it stays at ~0.52, the cleanliness mechanism is real and the judge earns its cost. Every downstream decision — judge in the loop or not — hangs on exactly this cell of the 2x2 you left empty.

One log correction to make regardless: the A/B as run compared *clean+small-menus* vs *dirty+huge-menus* with an asymmetric best-of-two retry (3 vs 0). Record it as confounded, not as evidence for the writer-poisoning mechanism.
